claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I'm asking about my three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I'd like to assess eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start by asking: **How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 7 years old, Blake is 20, and Charlie is also 7.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Now I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you.

Next question: **Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Now, regarding Blake who is 20 years old - **Is Blake currently in approved education or training?** (For context, to qualify for Child Benefit for someone aged 16-19, they need to be in approved education or training, but once they turn 20, Child Benefit normally stops.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training. Actually, Blake works full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you paying towards the care of Alex, Blake, and Charlie (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, of course - they all live with me so I pay for everything they need - food, clothes, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on your circumstances, here's my assessment:

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- ✅ **Alex** (age 7)
- ✅ **Charlie** (age 7)

**You are NOT eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- ❌ **Blake** (age 20)

**Explanation:**

For **Alex and Charlie**: Since they are both 7 years old (under 16), live with you in the UK, and you're responsible for their care, you meet all the requirements for Child Benefit.

For **Blake**: Unfortunately, Blake is 20 years old and working full-time. Child Benefit for children aged 16 or over is only available if they are under 20 **and** in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 and working full-time rather than being in education or training, you're not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

**Next Steps:**
You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie if you haven't already done so. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old, which is under 16. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's care (providing food, clothes, and other necessities). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria.

Blake is 20 years old and working full-time. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training - Blake is working full-time. Additionally, the rules state that you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria.

Charlie is 7 years old, which is under 16. The claimant lives in the UK, Charlie lives with them, and the claimant is responsible for Charlie's care (providing food, clothes, and other necessities). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria.

The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie as they are both under 16, live with the claimant, and the claimant provides for their care. However, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake, who is 20 years old and working full-time, as Child Benefit for children aged 16 or over requires them to be under 20 and in approved education or training.
